attacking_style: Opportunistic bridge specialist who responds to early selections rather than initiating attacks. Shows ability to survive multiple climb selections but lacks finishing kick to contest final sprint.
team_tactics: Limited data on team setup; appears to operate in breakaway scenarios where individual initiative matters more than team support. Lacks dedicated lead-out or positioning assistance in final.
terrain_preference: Suited to attritional breakaway scenarios with multiple selection points. Can handle climbing well enough to survive to final group but not explosive enough to create separation or sprint effectively.
vulnerability: Final sprint execution is a clear weakness - reached final quartet but couldn't contest podium. Lacks acceleration to bridge initial splits without sustained effort.
key_patterns: Missed initial breakaway formations but successfully bridged gaps through persistent chase efforts (2 observations).
Survived multi-climb selections to reach reduced final groups but failed to convert position into results (2 observations).
Operates primarily in breakaway contexts rather than peloton positioning or GC group dynamics (2 observations).
form_trajectory: neutral: Demonstrated consistent ability to reach advanced positions in breaks but no progression in converting presence to podium results across May 2022 Giro stages
